summary: A practical course for developers who already know Python, Java, or C# and need to master JavaScript and TypeScript — focusing on the mental model gaps and footguns that trip up experienced programmers.

modules.title: The JavaScript Landscape
modules.strata_theme: Host environments, runtime model, and what TypeScript actually is
modules.description: Understand what JavaScript is, where it runs, and how TypeScript relates to it — before writing a single line.
objectives: Distinguish the JavaScript language from its host environments (browser vs Node.js)
Explain what TypeScript adds to JavaScript and why types are erased at runtime
Identify the three layers: ECMAScript spec, V8 engine, host APIs

lessons.title: What Is JavaScript — and Why Does It Exist?
lessons.description: A developer-to-developer history of JavaScript: the 10-day origin story, ECMAScript standardization, TypeScript's emergence, and an honest comparison to Python, Java, Go, and Rust — before any code is written.

lessons.title: Runtimes, Engines, and Host Environments
lessons.description: The three-layer model of JS: language spec, engine, host APIs — and why the same code behaves differently in browser vs Node.js.

modules.title: Variables, Types & Coercion
modules.strata_theme: JS gotchas for experienced developers — scoping, types, and coercion
modules.description: The footguns that bite experienced developers on day one: var hoisting, TDZ, type coercion, and why == is broken.
objectives: Use let and const correctly and explain why var should be avoided
Apply strict equality (===) and explain when coercion surprises occur
Identify all 8 JS primitive types and their typeof quirks

lessons.title: var, let, and const — Scoping Pitfalls
lessons.description: How var hoisting and function scope break programmer intuition — and how let/const fix it.

lessons.title: Primitive Types and typeof
lessons.description: The 8 primitive types, the typeof operator, and the infamous typeof null === 'object' quirk.

lessons.title: Equality and Type Coercion
lessons.description: Why == is dangerous, how + causes string coercion, and the rules for always using ===.

modules.title: Collections & Iteration
modules.strata_theme: Arrays, objects, destructuring, and idiomatic modern JS iteration
modules.description: Work with arrays and objects using the functional methods and destructuring syntax that define modern JavaScript.
objectives: Use map, filter, reduce, find, and spread to manipulate arrays idiomatically
Apply object destructuring and spread in function parameters and assignments
Explain why for...in on arrays is a footgun and use for...of instead

lessons.title: Arrays and Functional Iteration
lessons.description: map, filter, reduce, find — the array methods that replace imperative loops in modern JS.

lessons.title: Objects and Destructuring
lessons.description: Object literals, shorthand properties, computed keys, spread, and destructuring in function parameters.

modules.title: Functions, Scope & Closures
modules.strata_theme: Lexical scope, closures, and the patterns they enable
modules.description: How JavaScript scoping and closures work — and why understanding this is a prerequisite for everything that follows.
objectives: Explain the difference between function declarations, expressions, and arrow functions
Demonstrate how closures capture lexical environments, not values
Fix the classic loop-var closure bug using let or factory functions

lessons.title: Function Forms
lessons.description: Declarations vs expressions vs arrow functions — hoisting differences and when each form is appropriate.

lessons.title: Lexical Scope
lessons.description: Scope is determined at write-time, not call-time — the foundation of closures and why this matters.

lessons.title: Closures
lessons.description: Closures in practice: the loop-var bug, factory functions, memoization, and data encapsulation.

modules.title: this Binding
modules.strata_theme: The this keyword — call-site rules and the most common source of bugs
modules.description: The single hardest concept for developers from Python/Java/C#. Five call-site rules, common traps, and the modern fixes.
objectives: Apply the five this call-site rules to predict this in any context
Diagnose and fix callback context-loss bugs in real code
Explain why this in JS differs fundamentally from self/this in Python/Java/C#

lessons.title: The Five this Call-Site Rules
lessons.description: Method, plain, arrow, explicit (call/apply/bind), and constructor calls — the complete mental model for this.

lessons.title: Common this Traps and Fixes
lessons.description: Three ways you lose this in real code — and three ways to fix it with bind, arrow fields, and wrapper arrows.

modules.title: Async JavaScript
modules.strata_theme: Event loop, Promises, and async/await — the complete async mental model
modules.description: Master the event loop model and write async code confidently — including parallel execution with Promise.all.
objectives: Explain the event loop, call stack, and the difference between the microtask and task queues
Chain Promises correctly with .then() and handle errors with .catch()
Use async/await with try/catch and Promise.all for sequential and parallel async code

lessons.title: The Event Loop
lessons.description: Call stack, task queue, microtask queue — the complete mental model for how JS handles async operations.

lessons.title: Promises
lessons.description: Promise states, .then() chaining, .catch() for error handling, Promise.all, Promise.any, Promise.allSettled.

lessons.title: async/await
lessons.description: async/await as syntactic sugar over Promises — try/catch for errors, parallel execution, and the 'always returns a Promise' gotcha.

modules.title: Modules & Tooling
modules.strata_theme: ES modules, CommonJS, tsc, and tsconfig — the compile pipeline
modules.description: Use ES modules in browser and Node.js, configure TypeScript, and understand the compilation pipeline.
objectives: Use ES module named and default exports/imports correctly
Configure tsconfig.json with the key options for strict, target, and module
Explain what tsc --noEmit does and why it's used in CI

lessons.title: Module Systems: ESM and CommonJS
lessons.description: Named vs default exports, ESM vs CJS interop, and the gotchas you'll encounter when mixing them.

lessons.title: TypeScript Configuration
lessons.description: tsconfig.json key options, strict mode flags, .d.ts declaration files, and @types/ packages.

modules.title: TypeScript Foundations
modules.strata_theme: Type inference, everyday types, structural typing — TS as a tool for intermediate JS devs
modules.description: Write TypeScript with confidence: type inference, everyday annotations, the structural type system, and how to work with strictNullChecks.
objectives: Use TypeScript's type inference to minimize annotations while retaining type safety
Write interface and type alias definitions and explain when to use each
Apply the structural type system to explain why TS accepts shapes, not names

lessons.title: Why TypeScript and How It Works
lessons.description: What TS catches that JS doesn't, type inference, erased types at runtime, and why noImplicitAny matters.

lessons.title: Everyday Types
lessons.description: string/number/boolean, arrays, tuples, objects, optional props, union types, null/undefined under strictNullChecks, literal types.

lessons.title: Interfaces, Type Aliases & Structural Typing
lessons.description: Interface vs type alias, structural typing (duck typing at compile time), type assertions, and the non-null assertion operator.

modules.title: TypeScript Advanced
modules.strata_theme: Generics, utility types, and discriminated unions — reusable, type-safe patterns
modules.description: Use generics, utility types, and discriminated unions to write reusable, type-safe code — including the Partial/Pick/Omit/ReturnType toolkit.
objectives: Write generic functions and interfaces with type constraints using extends and keyof
Apply Partial, Required, Readonly, Record, Pick, Omit, ReturnType, and Parameters utility types
Use discriminated unions with exhaustiveness checking to model variant data

lessons.title: Generics
lessons.description: Type parameters as variables, constrained generics with extends, keyof, and the identity function as entry point.

lessons.title: Utility Types
lessons.description: Partial, Required, Readonly, Record, Pick, Omit, NonNullable, ReturnType, Parameters, Awaited — practical uses for each.

lessons.title: Narrowing and Discriminated Unions
lessons.description: Type narrowing with typeof/in/instanceof, discriminated unions for variant data, never for exhaustiveness.

modules.title: DOM & Browser APIs
modules.strata_theme: The DOM as a host API — querying, mutating, and responding to events
modules.description: Manipulate the DOM and respond to user events without a framework — building the mental model that makes framework debugging possible.
objectives: Query and manipulate DOM nodes using querySelector, createElement, and classList
Attach event listeners and correctly handle event bubbling and delegation
Explain why DOM APIs are absent in Node.js and how that shapes architecture decisions

lessons.title: The DOM Tree and Manipulation
lessons.description: DOM as a host API, querySelector/querySelectorAll, createElement/appendChild/remove, textContent vs innerHTML (XSS note).

lessons.title: Events and Delegation
lessons.description: addEventListener, event object, bubbling, stopPropagation, event delegation as a performance pattern.

modules.title: Node.js Foundations
modules.strata_theme: Node as a runtime — process, built-in modules, HTTP server, and async I/O
modules.description: Write Node.js scripts and HTTP servers using built-in modules and modern ESM syntax.
objectives: Use process.env, process.argv, and built-in modules (node:fs, node:path) in Node.js scripts
Build a minimal HTTP server using node:http
Explain why Node.js suits I/O-heavy workloads and where its single-threaded model has limits

lessons.title: Node.js as a Runtime
lessons.description: V8 outside the browser, process object, node:fs and node:path, async file I/O with Promises, ESM in Node.

lessons.title: HTTP and the Server Model
lessons.description: Non-blocking I/O model, node:http createServer, environment-based config, and tsx for TypeScript in Node.

learning_plan.suggested_schedule: 3 sessions per week (3–4 lessons per session)
